title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXXXIX. Love And Matrimony.
content:
As I was going up Pippen-hill,
            Pippen-hill was dirty,
        There I met a pretty miss,
            And she dropt me a curtsey.

        Little miss, pretty miss,
            Blessings light upon you!
        If I had half-a-crown a day,
            I'd spend it all on you.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXXXIX. Love And Matrimony."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
